horizontal_fov._description: Horizontal field of view

image._description: The image size in pixels and format.

clip._description: The near and far clip planes. Objects closer or farther than these planes are not rendered.

near._description: Near clipping plane

far._description: Far clipping plane

save._description: Enable or disable saving of camera frames.

path._description: The path name which will hold the frame data. If path name is relative, then directory is relative to current working directory.

clip._description: The near and far clip planes. Objects closer or farther than these planes are not detected by the depth camera.

near._description: Near clipping plane for depth camera

far._description: Far clipping plane for depth camera

noise._description: The properties of the noise model that should be applied to generated images

type._description: The type of noise.  Currently supported types are: "gaussian" (draw additive noise values independently for each pixel from a Gaussian distribution).

mean._description: For type "gaussian," the mean of the Gaussian distribution from which noise values are drawn.

stddev._description: For type "gaussian," the standard deviation of the Gaussian distribution from which noise values are drawn.

distortion._description: Lens distortion to be applied to camera images. See http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Distortion_(optics)#Software_correction

center._description: The distortion center or principal point

type._description: Type of the lens mapping. Supported values are gnomonical, stereographic, equidistant, equisolid_angle, orthographic, custom. For gnomonical (perspective) projection, it is recommended to specify a horizontal_fov of less than or equal to 90°

scale_to_hfov._description: If true the image will be scaled to fit horizontal FOV, otherwise it will be shown according to projection type parameters

custom_function._description: Definition of custom mapping function in a form of r=c1*f*fun(theta/c2 + c3). See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fisheye_lens#Mapping_function

f._description: Focal length of the optical system. Note: It's not a focal length of the lens in a common sense! This value is ignored if 'scale_to_fov' is set to true

cutoff_angle._description: Everything outside of the specified angle will be hidden, 90° by default

env_texture_size._description: Resolution of the environment cube map used to draw the world

intrinsics._description: Camera intrinsic parameters for setting a custom perspective projection matrix (cannot be used with WideAngleCamera since this class uses image stitching from 6 different cameras for achieving a wide field of view). The focal lengths can be computed using focal_length_in_pixels = (image_width_in_pixels * 0.5) / tan(field_of_view_in_degrees * 0.5 * PI/180)

fx._description: X focal length (in pixels, overrides horizontal_fov)

fy._description: Y focal length (in pixels, overrides horizontal_fov)

contact._description: These elements are specific to the contact sensor.

collision._description: name of the collision element within a link that acts as the contact sensor.

topic._description: Topic on which contact data is published.

force_torque._description: These elements are specific to the force torque sensor.

frame._description:
Frame in which to report the wrench values. Currently supported frames are:
        "parent" report the wrench expressed in the orientation of the parent link frame,
        "child" report the wrench expressed in the orientation of the child link frame,
        "sensor" report the wrench expressed in the orientation of the joint sensor frame.
      Note that for each option the point with respect to which the
      torque component of the wrench is expressed is the joint origin.

measure_direction._description:
Direction of the wrench measured by the sensor. The supported options are:
        "parent_to_child" if the measured wrench is the one applied by the parent link on the child link,
        "child_to_parent" if the measured wrench is the one applied by the child link on the parent link.

imu._description: These elements are specific to the IMU sensor.

localization._description:
This string represents special hardcoded use cases that are commonly seen with typical robot IMU's:
          - CUSTOM: use Euler angle custom_rpy orientation specification.
                 The orientation of the IMU's reference frame is defined by adding the custom_rpy rotation
                 to the parent_frame.
          - NED: The IMU XYZ aligns with NED, where NED orientation relative to Gazebo world
                 is defined by the SphericalCoordinates class.
          - ENU: The IMU XYZ aligns with ENU, where ENU orientation relative to Gazebo world
                 is defined by the SphericalCoordinates class.
          - NWU: The IMU XYZ aligns with NWU, where NWU orientation relative to Gazebo world
                 is defined by the SphericalCoordinates class.
          - GRAV_UP: where direction of gravity maps to IMU reference frame Z-axis with Z-axis pointing in
                     the opposite direction of gravity. IMU reference frame X-axis direction is defined by grav_dir_x.
                     Note if grav_dir_x is parallel to gravity direction, this configuration fails.
                     Otherwise, IMU reference frame X-axis is defined by projection of grav_dir_x onto a plane
                     normal to the gravity vector. IMU reference frame Y-axis is a vector orthogonal to both
                     X and Z axis following the right hand rule.
          - GRAV_DOWN: where direction of gravity maps to IMU reference frame Z-axis with Z-axis pointing in
                       the direction of gravity. IMU reference frame X-axis direction is defined by grav_dir_x.
                       Note if grav_dir_x is parallel to gravity direction, this configuration fails.
                       Otherwise, IMU reference frame X-axis is defined by projection of grav_dir_x onto a plane
                       normal to the gravity vector. IMU reference frame Y-axis is a vector orthogonal to both
                       X and Z axis following the right hand rule.

custom_rpy._description:
This field and parent_frame are used when localization is set to CUSTOM.
        Orientation (fixed axis roll, pitch yaw) transform from parent_frame to this IMU's reference frame.
        Some common examples are:
          - IMU reports in its local frame on boot. IMU sensor frame is the reference frame.
             Example: parent_frame="", custom_rpy="0 0 0"
          - IMU reports in Gazebo world frame.
             Example sdf: parent_frame="world", custom_rpy="0 0 0"
          - IMU reports in NWU frame.
             Uses SphericalCoordinates class to determine world frame in relation to magnetic north and gravity;
             i.e. rotation between North-West-Up and world (+X,+Y,+Z) frame is defined by SphericalCoordinates class.
             Example sdf given world is NWU: parent_frame="world", custom_rpy="0 0 0"
          - IMU reports in NED frame.
             Uses SphericalCoordinates class to determine world frame in relation to magnetic north and gravity;
             i.e. rotation between North-East-Down and world (+X,+Y,+Z) frame is defined by SphericalCoordinates class.
             Example sdf given world is NWU: parent_frame="world", custom_rpy="M_PI 0 0"
          - IMU reports in ENU frame.
             Uses SphericalCoordinates class to determine world frame in relation to magnetic north and gravity;
             i.e. rotation between East-North-Up and world (+X,+Y,+Z) frame is defined by SphericalCoordinates class.
             Example sdf given world is NWU: parent_frame="world", custom_rpy="0 0 -0.5*M_PI"
          - IMU reports in ROS optical frame as described in http://www.ros.org/reps/rep-0103.html#suffix-frames, which is
             (z-forward, x-left to right when facing +z, y-top to bottom when facing +z).
             (default gazebo camera is +x:view direction, +y:left, +z:up).
             Example sdf: parent_frame="local", custom_rpy="-0.5*M_PI 0 -0.5*M_PI"

parent_frame.description:
Name of parent frame which the custom_rpy transform is defined relative to.
          It can be any valid fully scoped Gazebo Link name or the special reserved "world" frame.
          If left empty, use the sensor's own local frame.

grav_dir_x._description:
Used when localization is set to GRAV_UP or GRAV_DOWN, a projection of this vector
        into a plane that is orthogonal to the gravity vector
        defines the direction of the IMU reference frame's X-axis.
        grav_dir_x is  defined in the coordinate frame as defined by the parent_frame element.

parent_frame.description:
Name of parent frame in which the grav_dir_x vector is defined.
          It can be any valid fully scoped Gazebo Link name or the special reserved "world" frame.
          If left empty, use the sensor's own local frame.

topic._description: Topic on which data is published. DEPRECATED. Use the topic element that is a child of the sensor element.

angular_velocity._description:
These elements are specific to body-frame angular velocity,
    which is expressed in radians per second

x._description: Angular velocity about the X axis

noise._description: The properties of a sensor noise model.

type.description:
The type of noise. Currently supported types are:
      "none" (no noise).
      "gaussian" (draw noise values independently for each measurement from a Gaussian distribution).
      "gaussian_quantized" ("gaussian" plus quantization of outputs (ie. rounding))

mean._description:
For type "gaussian*", the mean of the Gaussian distribution from which
      noise values are drawn.

stddev._description: For type "gaussian*", the standard deviation of the Gaussian distribution from which noise values are drawn.

bias_mean._description: For type "gaussian*", the mean of the Gaussian distribution from which bias values are drawn.

bias_stddev._description: For type "gaussian*", the standard deviation of the Gaussian distribution from which bias values are drawn.

dynamic_bias_stddev._description: For type "gaussian*", the standard deviation of the noise used to drive a process to model slow variations in a sensor bias.

dynamic_bias_correlation_time._description: For type "gaussian*", the correlation time in seconds of the noise used to drive a process to model slow variations in a sensor bias. A typical value, when used, would be on the order of 3600 seconds (1 hour).

precision._description:
For type "gaussian_quantized", the precision of output signals. A value
      of zero implies infinite precision / no quantization.

lidar._description: These elements are specific to the lidar sensor.

samples._description: The number of simulated lidar rays to generate per complete laser sweep cycle.

resolution._description: This number is multiplied by samples to determine the number of range data points returned. If resolution is not equal to one, range data is interpolated.

max_angle._description: Must be greater or equal to min_angle

samples._description: The number of simulated lidar rays to generate per complete laser sweep cycle.

resolution._description: This number is multiplied by samples to determine the number of range data points returned. If resolution is not equal to one, range data is interpolated.

max_angle._description: Must be greater or equal to min_angle

range._description: specifies range properties of each simulated lidar

min._description: The minimum distance for each lidar ray.

max._description: The maximum distance for each lidar ray.

resolution._description: Linear resolution of each lidar ray.

noise._description: The properties of the noise model that should be applied to generated scans

type._description: The type of noise.  Currently supported types are: "gaussian" (draw noise values independently for each beam from a Gaussian distribution).

mean._description: For type "gaussian," the mean of the Gaussian distribution from which noise values are drawn.

stddev._description: For type "gaussian," the standard deviation of the Gaussian distribution from which noise values are drawn.

logical_camera._description: These elements are specific to logical camera sensors. A logical camera reports objects that fall within a frustum. Computation should be performed on the CPU.

near._description: Near clipping distance of the view frustum

far._description: Far clipping distance of the view frustum

aspect_ratio._description: Aspect ratio of the near and far planes. This is the width divided by the height of the near or far planes.

horizontal_fov._description: Horizontal field of view of the frustum, in radians. This is the angle between the frustum's vertex and the edges of the near or far plane.

ray._description: These elements are specific to the ray (laser) sensor.

samples._description: The number of simulated rays to generate per complete laser sweep cycle.

resolution._description: This number is multiplied by samples to determine the number of range data points returned. If resolution is less than one, range data is interpolated. If resolution is greater than one, range data is averaged.

max_angle._description: Must be greater or equal to min_angle

samples._description: The number of simulated rays to generate per complete laser sweep cycle.

resolution._description: This number is multiplied by samples to determine the number of range data points returned. If resolution is less than one, range data is interpolated. If resolution is greater than one, range data is averaged.

max_angle._description: Must be greater or equal to min_angle

range._description: specifies range properties of each simulated ray

min._description: The minimum distance for each ray.

max._description: The maximum distance for each ray.

resolution._description: Linear resolution of each ray.

noise._description: The properties of the noise model that should be applied to generated scans

type._description: The type of noise.  Currently supported types are: "gaussian" (draw noise values independently for each beam from a Gaussian distribution).

mean._description: For type "gaussian," the mean of the Gaussian distribution from which noise values are drawn.

stddev._description: For type "gaussian," the standard deviation of the Gaussian distribution from which noise values are drawn.

sonar._description: These elements are specific to the sonar sensor.

geometry._description: The sonar collision shape. Currently supported geometries are: "cone" and "sphere".

min._description: Minimum range

max._description: Max range

radius._description: Radius of the sonar cone at max range. This parameter is only used if geometry is "cone".

transceiver._description: These elements are specific to a wireless transceiver.

frequency._description: Specifies the frequency of transmission in MHz

min_frequency._description: Only a frequency range is filtered. Here we set the lower bound (MHz).

max_frequency._description: Only a frequency range is filtered. Here we set the upper bound (MHz).

gain._description: Specifies the antenna gain in dBi

power._description: Specifies the transmission power in dBm

sensitivity._description: Minimum received signal power in dBm

always_on._description: If true the sensor will always be updated according to the update rate.

update_rate._description: The frequency at which the sensor data is generated. If left unspecified, the sensor will generate data every cycle.

visualize._description: If true, the sensor is visualized in the GUI

topic._description: Name of the topic on which data is published. This is necessary for visualization

gearbox_ratio._description: Parameter for gearbox joints.  Given theta_1 and theta_2 defined in description for gearbox_reference_body, theta_2 = -gearbox_ratio * theta_1.

gearbox_reference_body._description: Parameter for gearbox joints.  Gearbox ratio is enforced over two joint angles.  First joint angle (theta_1) is the angle from the gearbox_reference_body to the parent link in the direction of the axis element and the second joint angle (theta_2) is the angle from the gearbox_reference_body to the child link in the direction of the axis2 element.

thread_pitch._description:
Parameter for screw joints representing the ratio between rotation
      and translation of the joint. This parameter has been interpreted by
      gazebo-classic as having units of radians / meter with a positive value
      corresponding to a left-handed thread.

axis._description:
Parameters related to the axis of rotation for revolute joints,
      the axis of translation for prismatic joints.

initial_position._description: Default joint position for this joint axis.

xyz._description:
Represents the x,y,z components of the axis unit vector. The axis is
        expressed in the joint frame unless the use_parent_model_frame
        flag is set to true. The vector should be normalized.

use_parent_model_frame._description:
Flag to interpret the axis xyz element in the parent model frame instead
        of joint frame. Provided for Gazebo compatibility
        (see https://github.com/osrf/gazebo/issue/494 ).

dynamics._description: An element specifying physical properties of the joint. These values are used to specify modeling properties of the joint, particularly useful for simulation.

damping._description: The physical velocity dependent viscous damping coefficient of the joint.

friction._description: The physical static friction value of the joint.

spring_reference._description: The spring reference position for this joint axis.

spring_stiffness._description: The spring stiffness for this joint axis.

limit._description: specifies the limits of this joint

lower._description: Specifies the lower joint limit (radians for revolute joints, meters for prismatic joints). Omit if joint is continuous.

upper._description: Specifies the upper joint limit (radians for revolute joints, meters for prismatic joints). Omit if joint is continuous.

effort._description: A value for enforcing the maximum joint effort applied. Limit is not enforced if value is negative.

velocity._description: A value for enforcing the maximum joint velocity.

stiffness._description: Joint stop stiffness.

dissipation._description: Joint stop dissipation.

axis2._description: Parameters related to the second axis of rotation for revolute2 joints and universal joints.

initial_position._description: Default joint position for this joint axis.

xyz._description:
Represents the x,y,z components of the axis unit vector. The axis is
        expressed in the joint frame unless the use_parent_model_frame
        flag is set to true. The vector should be normalized.

use_parent_model_frame._description:
Flag to interpret the axis xyz element in the parent model frame instead
        of joint frame. Provided for Gazebo compatibility
        (see https://github.com/osrf/gazebo/issue/494 ).

dynamics._description: An element specifying physical properties of the joint. These values are used to specify modeling properties of the joint, particularly useful for simulation.

damping._description: The physical velocity dependent viscous damping coefficient of the joint.  EXPERIMENTAL: if damping coefficient is negative and implicit_spring_damper is true, adaptive damping is used.

friction._description: The physical static friction value of the joint.

spring_reference._description: The spring reference position for this joint axis.

spring_stiffness._description: The spring stiffness for this joint axis.

lower._description: An attribute specifying the lower joint limit (radians for revolute joints, meters for prismatic joints). Omit if joint is continuous.

upper._description: An attribute specifying the upper joint limit (radians for revolute joints, meters for prismatic joints). Omit if joint is continuous.

effort._description: An attribute for enforcing the maximum joint effort applied by Joint::SetForce.  Limit is not enforced if value is negative.

velocity._description: (not implemented) An attribute for enforcing the maximum joint velocity.

stiffness._description: Joint stop stiffness. Supported physics engines: SimBody.

dissipation._description: Joint stop dissipation. Supported physics engines: SimBody.

physics._description: Parameters that are specific to a certain physics engine.

must_be_loop_joint._description: Force cut in the multibody graph at this joint.

provide_feedback._description: (DEPRECATION WARNING:  In SDFormat 1.5 this tag will be replaced by the same tag directly under the physics-block.  For now, this tag overrides the one outside of ode-block, but in SDFormat 1.5 this tag will be removed completely.)  If provide feedback is set to true, ODE will compute the constraint forces at this joint.

cfm_damping._description: If cfm damping is set to true, ODE will use CFM to simulate damping, allows for infinite damping, and one additional constraint row (previously used for joint limit) is always active.

implicit_spring_damper._description: If implicit_spring_damper is set to true, ODE will use CFM, ERP to simulate stiffness and damping, allows for infinite damping, and one additional constraint row (previously used for joint limit) is always active.  This replaces cfm_damping parameter in SDFormat 1.4.

fudge_factor._description: Scale the excess for in a joint motor at joint limits. Should be between zero and one.

cfm._description: Constraint force mixing for constrained directions

erp._description: Error reduction parameter for constrained directions

max_force._description: Maximum force or torque used to reach the desired velocity.

velocity._description: The desired velocity of the joint. Should only be set if you want the joint to move on load.

cfm._description: Constraint force mixing parameter used by the joint stop

erp._description: Error reduction parameter used by the joint stop

cfm._description: Suspension constraint force mixing parameter

erp._description: Suspension error reduction parameter

provide_feedback._description: If provide feedback is set to true, physics engine will compute the constraint forces at this joint.  For now, provide_feedback under ode block will override this tag and given user warning about the migration.  provide_feedback under ode is scheduled to be removed in SDFormat 1.5.

plugin._description: A plugin is a dynamically loaded chunk of code. It can exist as a child of world, model, and sensor.

name.description: A unique name for the plugin, scoped to its parent.

filename.description: Name of the shared library to load. If the filename is not a full path name, the file will be searched for in the configuration paths.

static._description: (DEPRECATION WARNING: This is deprecated in 1.6 and removed in 1.7. Actors should be static, so this is always true.

skin._description: Skin file which defines a visual and the underlying skeleton which moves it.

filename._description: Path to skin file, accepted formats: COLLADA, BVH.

scale._description: Scale the skin's size.

animation._description: Animation file defines an animation for the skeleton in the skin. The skeleton must be compatible with the skin skeleton.

name.description: Unique name for animation.

filename._description: Path to animation file. Accepted formats: COLLADA, BVH.

scale._description: Scale for the animation skeleton.

interpolate_x._description: Set to true so the animation is interpolated on X.

script._description: Adds scripted trajectories to the actor.

loop._description: Set this to true for the script to be repeated in a loop. For a fluid continuous motion, make sure the last waypoint matches the first one.

delay_start._description: This is the time to wait before starting the script. If running in a loop, this time will be waited before starting each cycle.

auto_start._description: Set to true if the animation should start as soon as the simulation starts playing. It is useful to set this to false if the animation should only start playing only when triggered by a plugin, for example.

trajectory._description: The trajectory contains a series of keyframes to be followed.

type.description: If it matches the type of an animation, they will be played at the same time.

tension.description: The tension of the trajectory spline. The default value of zero equates to a Catmull-Rom spline, which may also cause the animation to overshoot keyframes. A value of one will cause the animation to stick to the keyframes.

time._description: The time in seconds, counted from the beginning of the script, when the pose should be reached.

pose._description: The pose which should be reached at the given time.

plugin._description: A plugin is a dynamically loaded chunk of code. It can exist as a child of world, model, and sensor.

name.description: A unique name for the plugin, scoped to its parent.

filename.description: Name of the shared library to load. If the filename is not a full path name, the file will be searched for in the configuration paths.

joint._description: A joint connects two links with kinematic and dynamic properties. By default, the pose of a joint is expressed in the child link frame.

name.description: A unique name for the joint within the scope of the model.

type.description:
The type of joint, which must be one of the following:
      (continuous) a hinge joint that rotates on a single axis with a continuous range of motion,
      (revolute) a hinge joint that rotates on a single axis with a fixed range of motion,
      (gearbox) geared revolute joints,
      (revolute2) same as two revolute joints connected in series,
      (prismatic) a sliding joint that slides along an axis with a limited range specified by upper and lower limits,
      (ball) a ball and socket joint,
      (screw) a single degree of freedom joint with coupled sliding and rotational motion,
      (universal) like a ball joint, but constrains one degree of freedom,
      (fixed) a joint with zero degrees of freedom that rigidly connects two links.

sensor._description: The sensor tag describes the type and properties of a sensor.

name.description: A unique name for the sensor. This name must not match another model in the model.

type.description:
The type name of the sensor. By default, SDFormat supports types
                  air_pressure,
                  altimeter,
                  camera,
                  contact,
                  depth_camera, depth,
                  force_torque,
                  gps,
                  gpu_lidar,
                  gpu_ray,
                  imu,
                  lidar,
                  logical_camera,
                  magnetometer,
                  multicamera,
                  ray,
                  rfid,
                  rfidtag,
                  rgbd_camera, rgbd,
                  sonar,
                  thermal_camera, thermal,
                  wireless_receiver, and
                  wireless_transmitter.
      The "ray" and "gpu_ray" types are equivalent to "lidar" and "gpu_lidar", respectively. It is preferred to use "lidar" and "gpu_lidar" since "ray" and "gpu_ray" will be deprecated. The "ray" and "gpu_ray" types are maintained for legacy support.

plugin._description: A plugin is a dynamically loaded chunk of code. It can exist as a child of world, model, and sensor.

name.description: A unique name for the plugin, scoped to its parent.

filename.description: Name of the shared library to load. If the filename is not a full path name, the file will be searched for in the configuration paths.

air_pressure._description: These elements are specific to an air pressure sensor.

reference_altitude._description: The initial altitude in meters. This value can be used by a sensor implementation to augment the altitude of the sensor. For example, if you are using simulation instead of creating a 1000 m mountain model on which to place your sensor, you could instead set this value to 1000 and place your model on a ground plane with a Z height of zero.

pressure._description: Noise parameters for the pressure data.

noise._description: The properties of a sensor noise model.

type.description:
The type of noise. Currently supported types are:
      "none" (no noise).
      "gaussian" (draw noise values independently for each measurement from a Gaussian distribution).
      "gaussian_quantized" ("gaussian" plus quantization of outputs (ie. rounding))

mean._description:
For type "gaussian*", the mean of the Gaussian distribution from which
      noise values are drawn.

stddev._description: For type "gaussian*", the standard deviation of the Gaussian distribution from which noise values are drawn.

bias_mean._description: For type "gaussian*", the mean of the Gaussian distribution from which bias values are drawn.

bias_stddev._description: For type "gaussian*", the standard deviation of the Gaussian distribution from which bias values are drawn.

dynamic_bias_stddev._description: For type "gaussian*", the standard deviation of the noise used to drive a process to model slow variations in a sensor bias.

dynamic_bias_correlation_time._description: For type "gaussian*", the correlation time in seconds of the noise used to drive a process to model slow variations in a sensor bias. A typical value, when used, would be on the order of 3600 seconds (1 hour).

precision._description:
For type "gaussian_quantized", the precision of output signals. A value
      of zero implies infinite precision / no quantization.

image._description: The image size in pixels and format.

clip._description: The near and far clip planes. Objects closer or farther than these planes are not rendered.

near._description: Near clipping plane

far._description: Far clipping plane

save._description: Enable or disable saving of camera frames.

path._description: The path name which will hold the frame data. If path name is relative, then directory is relative to current working directory.

clip._description: The near and far clip planes. Objects closer or farther than these planes are not detected by the depth camera.

near._description: Near clipping plane for depth camera

far._description: Far clipping plane for depth camera

noise._description: The properties of the noise model that should be applied to generated images

type._description: The type of noise.  Currently supported types are: "gaussian" (draw additive noise values independently for each pixel from a Gaussian distribution).

mean._description: For type "gaussian," the mean of the Gaussian distribution from which noise values are drawn.

stddev._description: For type "gaussian," the standard deviation of the Gaussian distribution from which noise values are drawn.

distortion._description: Lens distortion to be applied to camera images. See http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Distortion_(optics)#Software_correction

center._description: The distortion center or principal point

type._description: Type of the lens mapping. Supported values are gnomonical, stereographic, equidistant, equisolid_angle, orthographic, custom. For gnomonical (perspective) projection, it is recommended to specify a horizontal_fov of less than or equal to 90°

scale_to_hfov._description: If true the image will be scaled to fit horizontal FOV, otherwise it will be shown according to projection type parameters

custom_function._description: Definition of custom mapping function in a form of r=c1*f*fun(theta/c2 + c3). See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fisheye_lens#Mapping_function

f._description: Focal length of the optical system. Note: It's not a focal length of the lens in a common sense! This value is ignored if 'scale_to_fov' is set to true

cutoff_angle._description: Everything outside of the specified angle will be hidden, 90° by default

env_texture_size._description: Resolution of the environment cube map used to draw the world

intrinsics._description: Camera intrinsic parameters for setting a custom perspective projection matrix (cannot be used with WideAngleCamera since this class uses image stitching from 6 different cameras for achieving a wide field of view). The focal lengths can be computed using focal_length_in_pixels = (image_width_in_pixels * 0.5) / tan(field_of_view_in_degrees * 0.5 * PI/180)

fx._description: X focal length (in pixels, overrides horizontal_fov)

fy._description: Y focal length (in pixels, overrides horizontal_fov)

contact._description: These elements are specific to the contact sensor.

collision._description: name of the collision element within a link that acts as the contact sensor.

topic._description: Topic on which contact data is published.

force_torque._description: These elements are specific to the force torque sensor.

frame._description:
Frame in which to report the wrench values. Currently supported frames are:
        "parent" report the wrench expressed in the orientation of the parent link frame,
        "child" report the wrench expressed in the orientation of the child link frame,
        "sensor" report the wrench expressed in the orientation of the joint sensor frame.
      Note that for each option the point with respect to which the
      torque component of the wrench is expressed is the joint origin.

measure_direction._description:
Direction of the wrench measured by the sensor. The supported options are:
        "parent_to_child" if the measured wrench is the one applied by the parent link on the child link,
        "child_to_parent" if the measured wrench is the one applied by the child link on the parent link.

imu._description: These elements are specific to the IMU sensor.

localization._description:
This string represents special hardcoded use cases that are commonly seen with typical robot IMU's:
          - CUSTOM: use Euler angle custom_rpy orientation specification.
                 The orientation of the IMU's reference frame is defined by adding the custom_rpy rotation
                 to the parent_frame.
          - NED: The IMU XYZ aligns with NED, where NED orientation relative to Gazebo world
                 is defined by the SphericalCoordinates class.
          - ENU: The IMU XYZ aligns with ENU, where ENU orientation relative to Gazebo world
                 is defined by the SphericalCoordinates class.
          - NWU: The IMU XYZ aligns with NWU, where NWU orientation relative to Gazebo world
                 is defined by the SphericalCoordinates class.
          - GRAV_UP: where direction of gravity maps to IMU reference frame Z-axis with Z-axis pointing in
                     the opposite direction of gravity. IMU reference frame X-axis direction is defined by grav_dir_x.
                     Note if grav_dir_x is parallel to gravity direction, this configuration fails.
                     Otherwise, IMU reference frame X-axis is defined by projection of grav_dir_x onto a plane
                     normal to the gravity vector. IMU reference frame Y-axis is a vector orthogonal to both
                     X and Z axis following the right hand rule.
          - GRAV_DOWN: where direction of gravity maps to IMU reference frame Z-axis with Z-axis pointing in
                       the direction of gravity. IMU reference frame X-axis direction is defined by grav_dir_x.
                       Note if grav_dir_x is parallel to gravity direction, this configuration fails.
                       Otherwise, IMU reference frame X-axis is defined by projection of grav_dir_x onto a plane
                       normal to the gravity vector. IMU reference frame Y-axis is a vector orthogonal to both
                       X and Z axis following the right hand rule.

custom_rpy._description:
This field and parent_frame are used when localization is set to CUSTOM.
        Orientation (fixed axis roll, pitch yaw) transform from parent_frame to this IMU's reference frame.
        Some common examples are:
          - IMU reports in its local frame on boot. IMU sensor frame is the reference frame.
             Example: parent_frame="", custom_rpy="0 0 0"
          - IMU reports in Gazebo world frame.
             Example sdf: parent_frame="world", custom_rpy="0 0 0"
          - IMU reports in NWU frame.
             Uses SphericalCoordinates class to determine world frame in relation to magnetic north and gravity;
             i.e. rotation between North-West-Up and world (+X,+Y,+Z) frame is defined by SphericalCoordinates class.
             Example sdf given world is NWU: parent_frame="world", custom_rpy="0 0 0"
          - IMU reports in NED frame.
             Uses SphericalCoordinates class to determine world frame in relation to magnetic north and gravity;
             i.e. rotation between North-East-Down and world (+X,+Y,+Z) frame is defined by SphericalCoordinates class.
             Example sdf given world is NWU: parent_frame="world", custom_rpy="M_PI 0 0"
          - IMU reports in ENU frame.
             Uses SphericalCoordinates class to determine world frame in relation to magnetic north and gravity;
             i.e. rotation between East-North-Up and world (+X,+Y,+Z) frame is defined by SphericalCoordinates class.
             Example sdf given world is NWU: parent_frame="world", custom_rpy="0 0 -0.5*M_PI"
          - IMU reports in ROS optical frame as described in http://www.ros.org/reps/rep-0103.html#suffix-frames, which is
             (z-forward, x-left to right when facing +z, y-top to bottom when facing +z).
             (default gazebo camera is +x:view direction, +y:left, +z:up).
             Example sdf: parent_frame="local", custom_rpy="-0.5*M_PI 0 -0.5*M_PI"

parent_frame.description:
Name of parent frame which the custom_rpy transform is defined relative to.
          It can be any valid fully scoped Gazebo Link name or the special reserved "world" frame.
          If left empty, use the sensor's own local frame.

grav_dir_x._description:
Used when localization is set to GRAV_UP or GRAV_DOWN, a projection of this vector
        into a plane that is orthogonal to the gravity vector
        defines the direction of the IMU reference frame's X-axis.
        grav_dir_x is  defined in the coordinate frame as defined by the parent_frame element.

parent_frame.description:
Name of parent frame in which the grav_dir_x vector is defined.
          It can be any valid fully scoped Gazebo Link name or the special reserved "world" frame.
          If left empty, use the sensor's own local frame.

samples._description: The number of simulated lidar rays to generate per complete laser sweep cycle.

range._description: specifies range properties of each simulated lidar

min._description: The minimum distance for each lidar ray.

max._description: The maximum distance for each lidar ray.

resolution._description: Linear resolution of each lidar ray.

noise._description: The properties of the noise model that should be applied to generated scans

type._description: The type of noise.  Currently supported types are: "gaussian" (draw noise values independently for each beam from a Gaussian distribution).

mean._description: For type "gaussian," the mean of the Gaussian distribution from which noise values are drawn.

stddev._description: For type "gaussian," the standard deviation of the Gaussian distribution from which noise values are drawn.

logical_camera._description: These elements are specific to logical camera sensors. A logical camera reports objects that fall within a frustum. Computation should be performed on the CPU.

near._description: Near clipping distance of the view frustum

far._description: Far clipping distance of the view frustum

aspect_ratio._description: Aspect ratio of the near and far planes. This is the width divided by the height of the near or far planes.

horizontal_fov._description: Horizontal field of view of the frustum, in radians. This is the angle between the frustum's vertex and the edges of the near or far plane.

magnetometer._description: These elements are specific to a Magnetometer sensor.

x._description: Parameters related to the body-frame X axis of the magnetometer

noise._description: The properties of a sensor noise model.

type.description:
The type of noise. Currently supported types are:
      "none" (no noise).
      "gaussian" (draw noise values independently for each measurement from a Gaussian distribution).
      "gaussian_quantized" ("gaussian" plus quantization of outputs (ie. rounding))

mean._description:
For type "gaussian*", the mean of the Gaussian distribution from which
      noise values are drawn.

stddev._description: For type "gaussian*", the standard deviation of the Gaussian distribution from which noise values are drawn.

bias_mean._description: For type "gaussian*", the mean of the Gaussian distribution from which bias values are drawn.

bias_stddev._description: For type "gaussian*", the standard deviation of the Gaussian distribution from which bias values are drawn.

dynamic_bias_stddev._description: For type "gaussian*", the standard deviation of the noise used to drive a process to model slow variations in a sensor bias.

dynamic_bias_correlation_time._description: For type "gaussian*", the correlation time in seconds of the noise used to drive a process to model slow variations in a sensor bias. A typical value, when used, would be on the order of 3600 seconds (1 hour).

precision._description:
For type "gaussian_quantized", the precision of output signals. A value
      of zero implies infinite precision / no quantization.

y._description: Parameters related to the body-frame Y axis of the magnetometer

noise._description: The properties of a sensor noise model.

precision._description:
For type "gaussian_quantized", the precision of output signals. A value

ray._description: These elements are specific to the ray (laser) sensor.

samples._description: The number of simulated rays to generate per complete laser sweep cycle.

resolution._description: This number is multiplied by samples to determine the number of range data points returned. If resolution is less than one, range data is interpolated. If resolution is greater than one, range data is averaged.

max_angle._description: Must be greater or equal to min_angle

samples._description: The number of simulated rays to generate per complete laser sweep cycle.

resolution._description: This number is multiplied by samples to determine the number of range data points returned. If resolution is less than one, range data is interpolated. If resolution is greater than one, range data is averaged.

max_angle._description: Must be greater or equal to min_angle

range._description: specifies range properties of each simulated ray

min._description: The minimum distance for each ray.

max._description: The maximum distance for each ray.

resolution._description: Linear resolution of each ray.

noise._description: The properties of the noise model that should be applied to generated scans

type._description: The type of noise.  Currently supported types are: "gaussian" (draw noise values independently for each beam from a Gaussian distribution).

mean._description: For type "gaussian," the mean of the Gaussian distribution from which noise values are drawn.

stddev._description: For type "gaussian," the standard deviation of the Gaussian distribution from which noise values are drawn.

sonar._description: These elements are specific to the sonar sensor.

geometry._description: The sonar collision shape. Currently supported geometries are: "cone" and "sphere".

min._description: Minimum range

max._description: Max range

radius._description: Radius of the sonar cone at max range. This parameter is only used if geometry is "cone".

transceiver._description: These elements are specific to a wireless transceiver.

frequency._description: Specifies the frequency of transmission in MHz

min_frequency._description: Only a frequency range is filtered. Here we set the lower bound (MHz).

max_frequency._description: Only a frequency range is filtered. Here we set the upper bound (MHz).

gain._description: Specifies the antenna gain in dBi

power._description: Specifies the transmission power in dBm

sensitivity._description: Minimum received signal power in dBm

always_on._description: If true the sensor will always be updated according to the update rate.

update_rate._description: The frequency at which the sensor data is generated. If left unspecified, the sensor will generate data every cycle.

visualize._description: If true, the sensor is visualized in the GUI

topic._description: Name of the topic on which data is published. This is necessary for visualization

gearbox_ratio._description: Parameter for gearbox joints.  Given theta_1 and theta_2 defined in description for gearbox_reference_body, theta_2 = -gearbox_ratio * theta_1.

gearbox_reference_body._description: Parameter for gearbox joints.  Gearbox ratio is enforced over two joint angles.  First joint angle (theta_1) is the angle from the gearbox_reference_body to the parent link in the direction of the axis element and the second joint angle (theta_2) is the angle from the gearbox_reference_body to the child link in the direction of the axis2 element.

thread_pitch._description:
Parameter for screw joints representing the ratio between rotation
      and translation of the joint. This parameter has been interpreted by
      gazebo-classic as having units of radians / meter with a positive value
      corresponding to a left-handed thread.

axis._description:
Parameters related to the axis of rotation for revolute joints,
      the axis of translation for prismatic joints.

initial_position._description: Default joint position for this joint axis.

xyz._description:
Represents the x,y,z components of the axis unit vector. The axis is
        expressed in the joint frame unless the use_parent_model_frame
        flag is set to true. The vector should be normalized.

use_parent_model_frame._description:
Flag to interpret the axis xyz element in the parent model frame instead
        of joint frame. Provided for Gazebo compatibility
        (see https://github.com/osrf/gazebo/issue/494 ).

dynamics._description: An element specifying physical properties of the joint. These values are used to specify modeling properties of the joint, particularly useful for simulation.

damping._description: The physical velocity dependent viscous damping coefficient of the joint.

friction._description: The physical static friction value of the joint.

spring_reference._description: The spring reference position for this joint axis.

spring_stiffness._description: The spring stiffness for this joint axis.

limit._description: specifies the limits of this joint

lower._description: Specifies the lower joint limit (radians for revolute joints, meters for prismatic joints). Omit if joint is continuous.

upper._description: Specifies the upper joint limit (radians for revolute joints, meters for prismatic joints). Omit if joint is continuous.

effort._description: A value for enforcing the maximum joint effort applied. Limit is not enforced if value is negative.

velocity._description: A value for enforcing the maximum joint velocity.

stiffness._description: Joint stop stiffness.

dissipation._description: Joint stop dissipation.

axis2._description: Parameters related to the second axis of rotation for revolute2 joints and universal joints.

initial_position._description: Default joint position for this joint axis.

xyz._description:
Represents the x,y,z components of the axis unit vector. The axis is
        expressed in the joint frame unless the use_parent_model_frame
        flag is set to true. The vector should be normalized.

use_parent_model_frame._description:
Flag to interpret the axis xyz element in the parent model frame instead
        of joint frame. Provided for Gazebo compatibility
        (see https://github.com/osrf/gazebo/issue/494 ).

dynamics._description: An element specifying physical properties of the joint. These values are used to specify modeling properties of the joint, particularly useful for simulation.

damping._description: The physical velocity dependent viscous damping coefficient of the joint.  EXPERIMENTAL: if damping coefficient is negative and implicit_spring_damper is true, adaptive damping is used.

friction._description: The physical static friction value of the joint.

spring_reference._description: The spring reference position for this joint axis.

spring_stiffness._description: The spring stiffness for this joint axis.

lower._description: An attribute specifying the lower joint limit (radians for revolute joints, meters for prismatic joints). Omit if joint is continuous.

upper._description: An attribute specifying the upper joint limit (radians for revolute joints, meters for prismatic joints). Omit if joint is continuous.

effort._description: An attribute for enforcing the maximum joint effort applied by Joint::SetForce.  Limit is not enforced if value is negative.

velocity._description: (not implemented) An attribute for enforcing the maximum joint velocity.

stiffness._description: Joint stop stiffness. Supported physics engines: SimBody.

dissipation._description: Joint stop dissipation. Supported physics engines: SimBody.

physics._description: Parameters that are specific to a certain physics engine.

must_be_loop_joint._description: Force cut in the multibody graph at this joint.

provide_feedback._description: (DEPRECATION WARNING:  In SDFormat 1.5 this tag will be replaced by the same tag directly under the physics-block.  For now, this tag overrides the one outside of ode-block, but in SDFormat 1.5 this tag will be removed completely.)  If provide feedback is set to true, ODE will compute the constraint forces at this joint.

cfm_damping._description: If cfm damping is set to true, ODE will use CFM to simulate damping, allows for infinite damping, and one additional constraint row (previously used for joint limit) is always active.

implicit_spring_damper._description: If implicit_spring_damper is set to true, ODE will use CFM, ERP to simulate stiffness and damping, allows for infinite damping, and one additional constraint row (previously used for joint limit) is always active.  This replaces cfm_damping parameter in SDFormat 1.4.

fudge_factor._description: Scale the excess for in a joint motor at joint limits. Should be between zero and one.

cfm._description: Constraint force mixing for constrained directions

erp._description: Error reduction parameter for constrained directions

max_force._description: Maximum force or torque used to reach the desired velocity.

velocity._description: The desired velocity of the joint. Should only be set if you want the joint to move on load.

cfm._description: Constraint force mixing parameter used by the joint stop

erp._description: Error reduction parameter used by the joint stop

cfm._description: Suspension constraint force mixing parameter

erp._description: Suspension error reduction parameter

provide_feedback._description: If provide feedback is set to true, physics engine will compute the constraint forces at this joint.  For now, provide_feedback under ode block will override this tag and given user warning about the migration.  provide_feedback under ode is scheduled to be removed in SDFormat 1.5.

name.description: Name of the road

material._description: The material of the visual element.

script._description: Name of material from an installed script file. This will override the color element if the script exists.

uri._description: URI of the material script file

name._description: Name of the script within the script file

type.description: vertex, pixel, normal_map_object_space, normal_map_tangent_space

normal_map._description: filename of the normal map

lighting._description: If false, dynamic lighting will be disabled

ambient._description: The ambient color of a material specified by set of four numbers representing red/green/blue, each in the range of [0,1].

diffuse._description: The diffuse color of a material specified by set of four numbers representing red/green/blue/alpha, each in the range of [0,1].

specular._description: The specular color of a material specified by set of four numbers representing red/green/blue/alpha, each in the range of [0,1].

emissive._description: The emissive color of a material specified by set of four numbers representing red/green/blue, each in the range of [0,1].

pbr._description: Physically Based Rendering (PBR) material. There are two PBR workflows: metal and specular. While both workflows and their parameters can be specified at the same time, typically only one of them will be used (depending on the underlying renderer capability). It is also recommended to use the same workflow for all materials in the world.

roughness._description: Material roughness in the range of [0,1], where 0 represents a smooth surface and 1 represents a rough surface. This is the inverse of a specular map in a PBR specular workflow.

metalness._description: Material metalness in the range of [0,1], where 0 represents non-metal and 1 represents raw metal

environment_map._description: Filename of the environment / reflection map, typically in the form of a cubemap

ambient_occlusion_map._description: Filename of the ambient occlusion map. The map defines the amount of ambient lighting on the surface.

normal_map._description: Filename of the normal map. The normals can be in the object space or tangent space as specified in the 'type' attribute

type.description: The space that the normals are in. Values are: 'object' or 'tangent'

glossiness._description: Material glossiness in the range of [0-1], where 0 represents a rough surface and 1 represents a smooth surface. This is the inverse of a roughness map in a PBR metal workflow.

environment_map._description: Filename of the environment / reflection map, typically in the form of a cubemap

ambient_occlusion_map._description: Filename of the ambient occlusion map. The map defines the amount of ambient lighting on the surface.

normal_map._description: Filename of the normal map. The normals can be in the object space or tangent space as specified in the 'type' attribute

type.description: The space that the normals are in. Values are: 'object' or 'tangent'

point._description: A series of points that define the path of the road.

surface_model._description:
Name of planetary surface model, used to determine the surface altitude
      at a given latitude and longitude. The default is an ellipsoid model of
      the earth based on the WGS-84 standard. It is used in Gazebo's GPS sensor
      implementation.

world_frame_orientation._description:
This field identifies how Gazebo world frame is aligned in Geographical
      sense.  The final Gazebo world frame orientation is obtained by rotating
      a frame aligned with following notation by the field heading_deg (Note
      that heading_deg corresponds to positive yaw rotation in the NED frame,
      so it's inverse specifies positive Z-rotation in ENU or NWU).
      Options are:
        - ENU (East-North-Up)
        - NED (North-East-Down)
        - NWU (North-West-Up)
      For example, world frame specified by setting world_orientation="ENU"
      and heading_deg=-90° is effectively equivalent to NWU with heading of 0°.

latitude_deg._description:
Geodetic latitude at origin of gazebo reference frame, specified
      in units of degrees.

longitude_deg._description:
Longitude at origin of gazebo reference frame, specified in units
      of degrees.

elevation._description: Elevation of origin of gazebo reference frame, specified in meters.

heading_deg._description:
Heading offset of gazebo reference frame, measured as angle between
      Gazebo world frame and the world_frame_orientation type (ENU/NED/NWU).
      Rotations about the downward-vector (e.g. North to East) are positive.
      The direction of rotation is chosen to be consistent with compass
      heading convention (e.g. 0 degrees points North and 90 degrees
      points East, positive rotation indicates counterclockwise rotation
      when viewed from top-down direction).
      The angle is specified in degrees.

wrench._description:
Force and torque applied to the link. The x, y, z components
      of the pose correspond to the force applied to the link, and the roll,
      pitch, yaw components correspond to the torque applied to the link

The link's mass, position of its center of mass, and its central inertia

inertia._description:
This link's moments of inertia ixx, iyy, izz and products of inertia
      ixy, ixz, iyz about Co (the link's center of mass) for the unit vectors
      Ĉx, Ĉy, Ĉᴢ fixed in the center-of-mass-frame C.
      Note: the orientation of Ĉx, Ĉy, Ĉᴢ relative to L̂x, L̂y, L̂ᴢ is specified
      by the `pose` tag.
      To avoid compatibility issues associated with the negative sign
      convention for product of inertia, align Ĉx, Ĉy, Ĉᴢ with principal
      inertia directions so that all the products of inertia are zero.
      For more information about this sign convention, see the following
      MathWorks documentation for working with CAD tools:
      https://www.mathworks.com/help/releases/R2021b/physmod/sm/ug/specify-custom-inertia.html#mw_b043ec69-835b-4ca9-8769-af2e6f1b190c

ixx._description: The link's moment of inertia about Co (the link's center of mass) for Ĉx.

ixy._description:
The link's product of inertia about Co (the link's center of mass) for
        Ĉx and Ĉy, where the product of inertia convention -m x y  (not +m x y)
        is used. If Ĉx or Ĉy is a principal inertia direction, ixy = 0.

ixz._description:
The link's product of inertia about Co (the link's center of mass) for
        Ĉx and Ĉz, where the product of inertia convention -m x z  (not +m x z)
        is used. If Ĉx or Ĉz is a principal inertia direction, ixz = 0.

iyy._description: The link's moment of inertia about Co (the link's center of mass) for Ĉy.

iyz._description:
The link's product of inertia about Co (the link's center of mass) for
        Ĉy and Ĉz, where the product of inertia convention -m y z  (not +m y z)
        is used. If Ĉy or Ĉz is a principal inertia direction, iyz = 0.

izz._description: The link's moment of inertia about Co (the link's center of mass) for Ĉz.

collision._description: The collision properties of a link. Note that this can be different from the visual properties of a link, for example, simpler collision models are often used to reduce computation time.

geometry._description: The shape of the visual or collision object.

size._description: The three side lengths of the box. The origin of the box is in its geometric center (inside the center of the box).

length._description: Length of the cylinder along the z axis

heightmap._description: A heightmap based on a 2d grayscale image.

uri._description: URI to a grayscale image file

size._description:
The size of the heightmap in world units.
      When loading an image: "size" is used if present, otherwise defaults to 1x1x1.
      When loading a DEM: "size" is used if present, otherwise defaults to true size of DEM.

texture._description: The heightmap can contain multiple textures. The order of the texture matters. The first texture will appear at the lowest height, and the last texture at the highest height. Use blend to control the height thresholds and fade between textures.

size._description: Size of the applied texture in meters.

normal._description: Normalmap texture image filename

blend._description: The blend tag controls how two adjacent textures are mixed. The number of blend elements should equal one less than the number of textures.

min_height._description: Min height of a blend layer

fade_dist._description: Distance over which the blend occurs

use_terrain_paging._description: Set if the rendering engine will use terrain paging

sampling._description: Samples per heightmap datum. For rasterized heightmaps, this indicates the number of samples to take per pixel. Using a lower value, e.g. 1, will generally improve the performance of the heightmap but lower the heightmap quality.

image._description: Extrude a set of boxes from a grayscale image.

uri._description: URI of the grayscale image file

scale._description: Scaling factor applied to the image

granularity._description: The amount of error in the model

uri._description: Mesh uri

submesh._description: Use a named submesh. The submesh must exist in the mesh specified by the uri

name._description: Name of the submesh within the parent mesh

center._description: Set to true to center the vertices of the submesh at 0,0,0. This will effectively remove any transformations on the submesh before the poses from parent links and models are applied.

scale._description: Scaling factor applied to the mesh

normal._description: Normal direction for the plane. When a Plane is used as a geometry for a Visual or Collision object, then the normal is specified in the Visual or Collision frame, respectively.

size._description: Length of each side of the plane. Note that this property is meaningful only for visualizing the Plane, i.e., when the Plane is used as a geometry for a Visual object. The Plane has infinite size when used as a geometry for a Collision object.

point._description: A series of points that define the path of the polyline.

empty._description: You can use the empty tag to make empty geometries.

restitution_coefficient._description: Bounciness coefficient of restitution, from [0...1], where 0=no bounciness.

threshold._description: Bounce capture velocity, below which effective coefficient of restitution is 0.

coefficient._description:
Torsional friction coefficient, unitless maximum ratio of
          tangential stress to normal stress.

use_patch_radius._description:
If this flag is true,
          torsional friction is calculated using the "patch_radius" parameter.
          If this flag is set to false,
          "surface_radius" (R) and contact depth (d)
          are used to compute the patch radius as sqrt(R*d).

surface_radius._description: Surface radius on the point of contact.

slip._description:
Force dependent slip for torsional friction,
            equivalent to inverse of viscous damping coefficient
            with units of rad/s/(Nm).
            A slip value of 0 is infinitely viscous.

mu._description:
Coefficient of friction in first friction pyramid direction,
          the unitless maximum ratio of force in first friction pyramid
          direction to normal force.

mu2._description:
Coefficient of friction in second friction pyramid direction,
          the unitless maximum ratio of force in second friction pyramid
          direction to normal force.

fdir1._description:
Unit vector specifying first friction pyramid direction in
          collision-fixed reference frame.
          If the friction pyramid model is in use,
          and this value is set to a unit vector for one of the
          colliding surfaces,
          the ODE Collide callback function will align the friction pyramid directions
          with a reference frame fixed to that collision surface.
          If both surfaces have this value set to a vector of zeros,
          the friction pyramid directions will be aligned with the world frame.
          If this value is set for both surfaces, the behavior is undefined.

slip1._description:
Force dependent slip in first friction pyramid direction,
          equivalent to inverse of viscous damping coefficient
          with units of m/s/N.
          A slip value of 0 is infinitely viscous.

slip2._description:
Force dependent slip in second friction pyramid direction,
          equivalent to inverse of viscous damping coefficient
          with units of m/s/N.
          A slip value of 0 is infinitely viscous.

friction._description:
Coefficient of friction in first friction pyramid direction,
          the unitless maximum ratio of force in first friction pyramid
          direction to normal force.

friction2._description:
Coefficient of friction in second friction pyramid direction,
          the unitless maximum ratio of force in second friction pyramid
          direction to normal force.

fdir1._description:
Unit vector specifying first friction pyramid direction in
          collision-fixed reference frame.
          If the friction pyramid model is in use,
          and this value is set to a unit vector for one of the
          colliding surfaces,
          the friction pyramid directions will be aligned
          with a reference frame fixed to that collision surface.
          If both surfaces have this value set to a vector of zeros,
          the friction pyramid directions will be aligned with the world frame.
          If this value is set for both surfaces, the behavior is undefined.

collide_without_contact._description: Flag to disable contact force generation, while still allowing collision checks and contact visualization to occur.

collide_without_contact_bitmask._description: Bitmask for collision filtering when collide_without_contact is on

collide_bitmask._description: Bitmask for collision filtering. This will override collide_without_contact. Parsed as 16-bit unsigned integer.

category_bitmask._description: Bitmask for category of collision filtering. Collision happens if ((category1 & collision2) | (category2 & collision1)) is not zero. If not specified, the category_bitmask should be interpreted as being the same as collide_bitmask. Parsed as 16-bit unsigned integer.

poissons_ratio._description:
Poisson's ratio is the unitless ratio between transverse and axial strain.
        This value must lie between (-1, 0.5).  Defaults to 0.3 for typical steel.
        Note typical silicone elastomers have Poisson's ratio near 0.49 ~ 0.50.

        For reference, approximate values for Material:(Young's Modulus, Poisson's Ratio)
        for some of the typical materials are:
          Plastic:  (1e8 ~ 3e9 Pa,  0.35 ~ 0.41),
          Wood:     (4e9 ~ 1e10 Pa, 0.22 ~ 0.50),
          Aluminum: (7e10 Pa,       0.32 ~ 0.35),
          Steel:    (2e11 Pa,       0.26 ~ 0.31).

elastic_modulus._description:
Young's Modulus in SI derived unit Pascal.
        Defaults to -1.  If value is less or equal to zero,
        contact using elastic modulus (with Poisson's Ratio) is disabled.

        For reference, approximate values for Material:(Young's Modulus, Poisson's Ratio)
        for some of the typical materials are:
          Plastic:  (1e8 ~ 3e9 Pa,  0.35 ~ 0.41),
          Wood:     (4e9 ~ 1e10 Pa, 0.22 ~ 0.50),
          Aluminum: (7e10 Pa,       0.32 ~ 0.35),
          Steel:    (2e11 Pa,       0.26 ~ 0.31).

max_vel._description: maximum contact correction velocity truncation term.

min_depth._description: minimum allowable depth before contact correction impulse is applied

split_impulse._description: Similar to ODE's max_vel implementation.  See http://bulletphysics.org/mediawiki-1.5.8/index.php/BtContactSolverInfo#Split_Impulse for more information.

split_impulse_penetration_threshold._description: Similar to ODE's max_vel implementation.  See http://bulletphysics.org/mediawiki-1.5.8/index.php/BtContactSolverInfo#Split_Impulse for more information.

dart._description:
soft contact pamameters based on paper:
             http://www.cc.gatech.edu/graphics/projects/Sumit/homepage/papers/sigasia11/jain_softcontacts_siga11.pdf

bone_attachment._description: This is variable k_v in the soft contacts paper.  Its unit is N/m.

stiffness._description: This is variable k_e in the soft contacts paper.  Its unit is N/m.

damping._description: Viscous damping of point velocity in body frame.  Its unit is N/m/s.

flesh_mass_fraction._description: Fraction of mass to be distributed among deformable nodes.

laser_retro._description: intensity value returned by laser sensor.

max_contacts._description: Maximum number of contacts allowed between two entities. This value overrides the max_contacts element defined in physics.

visual._description: The visual properties of the link. This element specifies the shape of the object (box, cylinder, etc.) for visualization purposes.

name.description: Unique name for the visual element within the scope of the parent link.

frame._description: A frame of reference to which a pose is relative.

name.description: Name of the frame. This name must not match another frame defined inside the parent that this frame is attached to.

pose._description: A position(x,y,z) and orientation(roll, pitch yaw) with respect to the specified frame.

frame.description: Name of frame which the pose is defined relative to.

pose._description: A position(x,y,z) and orientation(roll, pitch yaw) with respect to the specified frame.

frame.description: Name of frame which the pose is defined relative to.

material._description: The material of the visual element.

script._description: Name of material from an installed script file. This will override the color element if the script exists.

uri._description: URI of the material script file

name._description: Name of the script within the script file

type.description: vertex, pixel, normal_map_object_space, normal_map_tangent_space

normal_map._description: filename of the normal map

lighting._description: If false, dynamic lighting will be disabled

ambient._description: The ambient color of a material specified by set of four numbers representing red/green/blue, each in the range of [0,1].

diffuse._description: The diffuse color of a material specified by set of four numbers representing red/green/blue/alpha, each in the range of [0,1].

specular._description: The specular color of a material specified by set of four numbers representing red/green/blue/alpha, each in the range of [0,1].

emissive._description: The emissive color of a material specified by set of four numbers representing red/green/blue, each in the range of [0,1].

pbr._description: Physically Based Rendering (PBR) material. There are two PBR workflows: metal and specular. While both workflows and their parameters can be specified at the same time, typically only one of them will be used (depending on the underlying renderer capability). It is also recommended to use the same workflow for all materials in the world.

roughness._description: Material roughness in the range of [0,1], where 0 represents a smooth surface and 1 represents a rough surface. This is the inverse of a specular map in a PBR specular workflow.

metalness._description: Material metalness in the range of [0,1], where 0 represents non-metal and 1 represents raw metal

environment_map._description: Filename of the environment / reflection map, typically in the form of a cubemap

ambient_occlusion_map._description: Filename of the ambient occlusion map. The map defines the amount of ambient lighting on the surface.

normal_map._description: Filename of the normal map. The normals can be in the object space or tangent space as specified in the 'type' attribute

type.description: The space that the normals are in. Values are: 'object' or 'tangent'

glossiness._description: Material glossiness in the range of [0-1], where 0 represents a rough surface and 1 represents a smooth surface. This is the inverse of a roughness map in a PBR metal workflow.

environment_map._description: Filename of the environment / reflection map, typically in the form of a cubemap

ambient_occlusion_map._description: Filename of the ambient occlusion map. The map defines the amount of ambient lighting on the surface.

normal_map._description: Filename of the normal map. The normals can be in the object space or tangent space as specified in the 'type' attribute

type.description: The space that the normals are in. Values are: 'object' or 'tangent'

geometry._description: The shape of the visual or collision object.

size._description: The three side lengths of the box. The origin of the box is in its geometric center (inside the center of the box).

length._description: Length of the cylinder along the z axis

heightmap._description: A heightmap based on a 2d grayscale image.

uri._description: URI to a grayscale image file

size._description:
The size of the heightmap in world units.
      When loading an image: "size" is used if present, otherwise defaults to 1x1x1.
      When loading a DEM: "size" is used if present, otherwise defaults to true size of DEM.

texture._description: The heightmap can contain multiple textures. The order of the texture matters. The first texture will appear at the lowest height, and the last texture at the highest height. Use blend to control the height thresholds and fade between textures.

size._description: Size of the applied texture in meters.

normal._description: Normalmap texture image filename

blend._description: The blend tag controls how two adjacent textures are mixed. The number of blend elements should equal one less than the number of textures.

min_height._description: Min height of a blend layer

fade_dist._description: Distance over which the blend occurs

use_terrain_paging._description: Set if the rendering engine will use terrain paging

sampling._description: Samples per heightmap datum. For rasterized heightmaps, this indicates the number of samples to take per pixel. Using a lower value, e.g. 1, will generally improve the performance of the heightmap but lower the heightmap quality.

image._description: Extrude a set of boxes from a grayscale image.

uri._description: URI of the grayscale image file

scale._description: Scaling factor applied to the image

granularity._description: The amount of error in the model

uri._description: Mesh uri

submesh._description: Use a named submesh. The submesh must exist in the mesh specified by the uri

name._description: Name of the submesh within the parent mesh

center._description: Set to true to center the vertices of the submesh at 0,0,0. This will effectively remove any transformations on the submesh before the poses from parent links and models are applied.

scale._description: Scaling factor applied to the mesh

normal._description: Normal direction for the plane. When a Plane is used as a geometry for a Visual or Collision object, then the normal is specified in the Visual or Collision frame, respectively.

size._description: Length of each side of the plane. Note that this property is meaningful only for visualizing the Plane, i.e., when the Plane is used as a geometry for a Visual object. The Plane has infinite size when used as a geometry for a Collision object.

point._description: A series of points that define the path of the polyline.

empty._description: You can use the empty tag to make empty geometries.

plugin._description: A plugin is a dynamically loaded chunk of code. It can exist as a child of world, model, and sensor.

name.description: A unique name for the plugin, scoped to its parent.

filename.description: Name of the shared library to load. If the filename is not a full path name, the file will be searched for in the configuration paths.

cast_shadows._description: If true the visual will cast shadows.

laser_retro._description: will be implemented in the future release.

transparency._description: The amount of transparency( 0=opaque, 1 = fully transparent)

meta._description: Optional meta information for the visual. The information contained within this element should be used to provide additional feedback to an end user.

layer._description: The layer in which this visual is displayed. The layer number is useful for programs, such as Gazebo, that put visuals in different layers for enhanced visualization.

sensor._description: The sensor tag describes the type and properties of a sensor.

name.description: A unique name for the sensor. This name must not match another model in the model.

type.description:
The type name of the sensor. By default, SDFormat supports types
                  air_pressure,
                  altimeter,
                  camera,
                  contact,
                  depth_camera, depth,
                  force_torque,
                  gps,
                  gpu_lidar,
                  gpu_ray,
                  imu,
                  lidar,
                  logical_camera,
                  magnetometer,
                  multicamera,
                  ray,
                  rfid,
                  rfidtag,
                  rgbd_camera, rgbd,
                  sonar,
                  thermal_camera, thermal,
                  wireless_receiver, and
                  wireless_transmitter.
      The "ray" and "gpu_ray" types are equivalent to "lidar" and "gpu_lidar", respectively. It is preferred to use "lidar" and "gpu_lidar" since "ray" and "gpu_ray" will be deprecated. The "ray" and "gpu_ray" types are maintained for legacy support.

frame._description: A frame of reference to which a pose is relative.

name.description: Name of the frame. This name must not match another frame defined inside the parent that this frame is attached to.

frame.description: Name of frame which the pose is defined relative to.

filename.description: Name of the shared library to load. If the filename is not a full path name, the file will be searched for in the configuration paths.

air_pressure._description: These elements are specific to an air pressure sensor.

reference_altitude._description: The initial altitude in meters. This value can be used by a sensor implementation to augment the altitude of the sensor. For example, if you are using simulation instead of creating a 1000 m mountain model on which to place your sensor, you could instead set this value to 1000 and place your model on a ground plane with a Z height of zero.

pressure._description: Noise parameters for the pressure data.

noise._description: The properties of a sensor noise model.

type.description:
The type of noise. Currently supported types are:
      "none" (no noise).
      "gaussian" (draw noise values independently for each measurement from a Gaussian distribution).
      "gaussian_quantized" ("gaussian" plus quantization of outputs (ie. rounding))

mean._description:
For type "gaussian*", the mean of the Gaussian distribution from which
      noise values are drawn.

stddev._description: For type "gaussian*", the standard deviation of the Gaussian distribution from which noise values are drawn.

bias_mean._description: For type "gaussian*", the mean of the Gaussian distribution from which bias values are drawn.

bias_stddev._description: For type "gaussian*", the standard deviation of the Gaussian distribution from which bias values are drawn.

dynamic_bias_stddev._description: For type "gaussian*", the standard deviation of the noise used to drive a process to model slow variations in a sensor bias.

dynamic_bias_correlation_time._description: For type "gaussian*", the correlation time in seconds of the noise used to drive a process to model slow variations in a sensor bias. A typical value, when used, would be on the order of 3600 seconds (1 hour).

precision._description:
For type "gaussian_quantized", the precision of output signals. A value
      of zero implies infinite precision / no quantization.
